LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINSTRATION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS, BRIGID HANSON, RODERICK HICKMAN AND TIM VIRGA'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES PURSUANT TO 42 U.S.C. SEC 1988 IS DENIED. DEFENDANT IS CORRECT THAT 42 U.S.C. SEC 1988 PROVIDES A SEPARATE BASIS APART FROM CCP 1038 FOR DEFENDANT TO SEEK ATTORNEYS' FEES HAVING PREVAILED IN THIS ACTION. HOWEVER, DEFENDANT MAY NOT MAKE SERIATIM MOTIONS THAT SEEK THE SAME RELIEF; RATHER DEFENDANT WAS OBLIGATED TO PUT FORTH ALL OF ITS REASONS FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEYS' FEES WHEN DEFENDANT MADE THE INITIAL REQUEST. THIS IS THE TEACHING OF BALDWIN V. HOME SAVINGS OF AMERICA (1997) 59 CAL. APP. 4TH 1192. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Sep-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Laurie Hepler of Carroll Burdick & McDonough (989.5900) for plaintiffs and responding parties
Richard Rahm and Joshua Kienitz of Littler Mendelson (433.1940) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PERSONNEL ADMINSTRATION, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS, BRIGID HANSON, RODERICK HICKMAN AND TIM VIRGA'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees Pursuant To 42 U.S.C. Sec 1988 is denied. Defendant is correct that 42 U.S.C. Sec 1988 provides a separate basis apart from CCP 1038 for defendant to seek attorneys' fees having prevailed in this action. However, defendant may not make seriatim motions that seek the same relief; rather defendant was obligated to put forth all of its reasons for an award of attorneys' fees when defendant made the initial request. This is the teaching of Baldwin v. Home Savings of America (1997) 59 Cal. App. 4th 1192. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
